skill.name: sql-queries
skill.description: Expert SQL query generation for DBX Studio. Use when writing, optimizing, or debugging SQL queries against user database connections.
skill.summary: Expert SQL query generation for DBX Studio database operations.
skill.author: Dbxstudio

security_audit.summary: All 24 static findings are false positives. Backtick patterns are Markdown code block delimiters (```sql) for SQL syntax highlighting, not shell command execution. Weak crypto findings are SQL keywords (ROW_NUMBER, DATE_TRUNC) in documentation examples. This skill contains only documentation and SQL query patterns with no executable code.

content.user_title: Generate SQL Queries for Database Analysis
content.value_statement: Writing complex SQL queries from scratch is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill provides expert SQL patterns and tool usage guidance for DBX Studio to generate accurate, optimized queries across multiple database dialects.
actual_capabilities: Generate SELECT queries with proper syntax for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks
Create complex queries using Common Table Expressions (CTEs) and window functions
Write aggregation queries with GROUP BY and date/time functions
Use DBX Studio AI tools (read_schema, execute_query, describe_table) to validate queries before execution
Apply proper identifier quoting (schema.table.column) to prevent syntax errors
Automatically add LIMIT clauses to prevent accidental full-table scans
limitations: Only SELECT and WITH (CTE) queries are permitted via execute_query tool for safety
Cannot execute INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DDL statements through this skill
Database-specific syntax varies across dialects - skill provides guidance but may need adjustment
Requires active database connection and appropriate permissions in DBX Studio
Does not replace need for understanding database schema and relationships

use_cases.title: Data Analyst Querying Sales Data
use_cases.description: Generate SQL queries to analyze monthly sales trends, calculate revenue metrics, and rank products by performance using window functions and aggregations.

use_cases.title: Developer Debugging Database Performance
use_cases.description: Write optimized queries to identify slow operations, analyze table statistics, and test index strategies without writing raw SQL from scratch.

use_cases.title: Business User Building Custom Reports
use_cases.description: Create complex queries joining multiple tables to generate custom reports on customer behavior, order patterns, and inventory levels.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Data Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the top 10 customers by total order value from the orders table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner-friendly request for basic ranking query

prompt_templates.title: Time-Series Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate daily revenue trends for the past 90 days, including moving averages and week-over-week comparisons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate query with date functions and window analytics

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Table CTE Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Find customers who made purchases in Q1 but not Q2, including their first purchase date and total spend across all categories.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced query using CTEs, joins, and conditional aggregation

prompt_templates.title: Performance Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the query execution plan for the slow dashboard loader and suggest optimizations for the date_range table join.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert-level debugging and query optimization task

output_examples.input: How many orders were placed each month last year?
output_examples.output: There were 1,247 orders in January, 1,892 in February, 2,156 in March, continuing through December with 3,421 orders. Peak month was December with 3,421 orders.

output_examples.input: Show me the top 5 products by revenue
output_examples.output: The top 5 products by revenue are: Premium Widget Kit ($124,500), Enterprise License ($98,200), Professional Subscription ($87,300), Support Package ($65,400), and Training Course ($54,100).

best_practices: Always use read_schema or describe_table tools before writing queries to validate table and column names exist
Quote all identifiers with double quotes (schema.table.column) to handle reserved words and case sensitivity
Add LIMIT clauses automatically unless the user explicitly requests all rows to prevent accidental large result sets
Use CTEs (WITH clauses) for complex queries to improve readability and enable recursive patterns
anti_patterns: Writing queries without first validating the schema using read_schema or describe_table tools
Generating INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DROP statements - only SELECT and WITH are permitted via execute_query
Omitting LIMIT clauses on exploratory queries which can cause timeouts on large tables
Using unquoted identifiers which fails on tables with mixed-case names or reserved keywords

faq.question: Which database dialects are supported?
faq.answer: PostgreSQL is the default. The skill also supports Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, MySQL, and SQLite via connection strings or MCP connectors.

faq.question: Can I modify data with this skill?
faq.answer: No. The execute_query tool only permits SELECT and WITH (CTE) queries for safety. INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and DDL operations are blocked.

faq.question: Why should I quote table and column names?
faq.answer: Quoting (schema.table.column) prevents errors from reserved keywords, handles case-sensitive names, and ensures compatibility across database dialects.

faq.question: How do I avoid timeout errors on large tables?
faq.answer: Always add LIMIT clauses to queries. Start with small limits to test query logic, then increase if needed. Use get_table_stats first to understand row counts.

faq.question: What tools should I use when analyzing a new table?
faq.answer: Start with read_schema to see all tables, then describe_table for column details and foreign keys. Use get_table_data to preview rows before writing complex queries.

faq.question: Can this skill help with query optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides patterns for efficient queries including CTEs, window functions, and proper indexing strategies. It can analyze existing queries and suggest improvements.
